microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's enterprise resilience validation program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized infrastructure**, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across strategic accounts including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, production-grade model trusted by enterprise customers.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation model across distributed cloud infrastructure.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud **network isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, navigating compliance requirements and multi-stakeholder coordination across platform and infra teams.

Built an internal AI agent and restructured drill-planning workflows to cut planning cycle time by **39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, directly expanding the team's ability to support high-velocity enterprise account commitments.
microsoft_2023: Drove **AI infrastructure adoption** across 14 Azure service teams by leading technical demos and hands-on training for AI-driven code generation workflows, saving **37 engineering hours monthly** and establishing repeatable enablement playbooks.

Shaped the product roadmap toward intent-based YAML generation by conducting **11+ technical discovery sessions** with Azure engineering stakeholders, surfacing critical feature gaps and translating findings into structured requirements for the platform team.

Accelerated AI-powered search adoption by migrating documentation to a **semantic search platform**, enforcing metadata standards that cut lookup time by 83% and improved retrieval accuracy across distributed engineering teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, orchestrating buy-in across 140+ engineering and infrastructure teams.

Conducted deep technical discovery with 20+ Azure service teams to map **81 hours of manual toil per region launch**, then built a prioritized roadmap to automate critical deployment paths and reduce operational burden at scale.

Engineered a **Power BI infrastructure dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling engineering leadership to identify automation gaps, prioritize high-impact investments, and allocate resources across cloud migration workstreams.

Delivered a cross-functional automation strategy that unified DevOps, platform, and infrastructure stakeholders around shared OKRs, accelerating cloud infrastructure modernization and establishing a repeatable execution model for future region rollouts.
amazon_robotics: Architected a **zero-downtime OS migration strategy** for a 2,000+ unit pilot fleet, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations and coordinating cross-functional execution across IT, Operations, and Engineering to hit production-grade reliability targets.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process across distributed robotics infrastructure teams.

Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual handoffs across the distributed systems stack.

Defined dependency mapping and rollout sequencing for a large-scale infrastructure migration, applying distributed systems thinking to ensure platform continuity and production-grade scalability throughout a multi-phase transition.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end sales operations lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a new CRM process that improved pipeline visibility and accelerated close rates across enterprise accounts.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and search profiles to improve top-of-funnel performance.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying and sealing margin leaks that had been obscured by manual, unstructured data across the business.

tailoring_notes: 'AI infrastructure' and 'production-grade scalability' from the JD woven into microsoft_ft bullets #1 and #2, framing Azure resilience work as enterprise infrastructure platform ownership.

'Technical discovery' (JD: 'deep technical discovery across platform, ML, DevOps, and infrastructure stakeholders') surfaced explicitly in microsoft_2023 bullet #2 and microsoft_2022 bullet #2, mirroring SA discovery-led selling motion.

'Cloud infrastructure modernization' and 'migration strategy' (JD: 'architect end-to-end migration strategies from AWS/GCP/Azure') introduced in microsoft_2022 bullets #3 and #4 and amazon_robotics bullet #1 to show migration execution experience.

'Network isolation' and 'compliance' (JD: 'navigate security reviews, compliance discussions') called out explicitly in microsoft_ft bullet #4, tied to a named $1.5B+ enterprise contract to signal 7-8 figure account experience.

'Distributed systems' and 'CI/CD' (JD requirements) reinforced in amazon_robotics bullets #3 and #4, connecting robotics infrastructure work to Modal's serverless compute and container orchestration context.
